element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns Donkey/Diddy movement differentiation (Donkey stronger, Diddy faster)
Hit by obstacle = active Kong runs off, player controls alternate Kong; rescuing requires freeing from barrel
Core movement tech; grants speed boost, cancellable with jump; rolling through enemies extends roll without stop animation
Defeated by jumping on, rolling into, or barrel-throwing; varied enemy placement increases difficulty
Both pickup/throw for damage and cannon barrels for launching into level sections
Unique level mechanic requiring timed jumps and obstacle avoidance on track
Launches Kongs out of barrel cannons; directional puzzle element
Jumping off ropes grants directional momentum; faster than climbing; DKC1 excels at single vertical ropes
Grant special abilities similar to Yoshi; must free from barrel to use
Large enemy requiring pattern recognition and accumulated move mastery
Hidden collectibles rewarding exploration; tied to level completion tracking

element_introduction_order.element: Terrain variety (jungle, underwater, cave, mine, mountain, factory)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Each theme introduces environment-specific hazards and mechanics

typical_relief_triggers: Safe platform stretches between enemy gauntlets
Kong swap after damage (brief invulnerability frame)
Checkpoint barrels freeing trapped partner Kong
Animal buddy activation granting temporary protection
typical_tension_triggers: Underwater sections with oxygen management implied
Rapid enemy waves in factory/industrial levels
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Graduated difficulty with alternating safe/danger zones; early levels establish core mechanics in low-stress environments; mid-game compounds mechanics with tighter enemy spacing; late-game combines multiple introduced systems (minecarts+enemies, ropes+hazards) creating sustained tension plateaus broken by checkpoint relief

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Barrel placements and raised platforms hint toward secret bonus areas; visual environmental density suggests off-path routes
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Bonus stages hidden off main path; discoverable through barrel-breaking and alternative route traversal

difficulty_curve.early_game: Low-stress introduction to Donkey/Diddy mechanics, basic enemy patterns, safe platforms with ample space; jungle theme provides generous level design
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Introduces minecarts, rope sections, animal buddies; enemy density increases; level themes diversify (underwater, cave, mine); mechanics begin compounding
difficulty_curve.late_game: Factory/mountain settings; tight enemy gauntlets; multiple mechanic combinations in single levels; boss encounters require full mechanic mastery
notable_spikes: First minecart level (adjustment to new locomotion mode)
Introduction of rapid rope sequences (fine timing required)
Underwater levels (inverted controls or oxygen tension)
Factory levels (sustained enemy pressure + hazard density)
Final boss encounter (synthesis of all mechanics)

notable_level_patterns.description: Levels alternate between open, mechanic-teaching sections and enemy-dense gauntlets, with relief zones between danger peaks
Early levels in each environment introduce new mechanics (minecarts, ropes, animals) in isolated, forgiving contexts before combining with existing hazards
Rope and climbing sections reward precise jumping technique; momentum management is critical; DKC1 emphasizes single vertical rope mastery
Late-game levels stack multiple obstacle types (enemies + spikes + narrow platforms + time pressure) requiring simultaneous execution of multiple learned skills
Each world ends with large enemy encounter testing pattern recognition and mechanic synthesis from that world's teaching
